reason: User initialization files (e.g., .profile, .bashrc, .bash_login) are executed during user login and can be leveraged for persistence if modified by unauthorized users. Setting the mode to 0740 restricts write access to the owner and read/execute to group, preventing unauthorized alteration while preserving necessary functionality.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Unauthorized modification of user initialization files; Potential privilege escalation; Persistence mechanisms
